api_endpoint.description: The URL to use for the DigitalOcean API.

http_retry_max.description: The maximum number of retries on a failed API request.

http_retry_wait_max.description: The maximum wait time (in seconds) between failed API requests.

http_retry_wait_min.description: The minimum wait time (in seconds) between failed API requests.

requests_per_second.description: The rate of requests per second to limit the HTTP client.

spaces_access_id.description: The access key ID for Spaces API operations.

spaces_endpoint.description: The URL to use for the DigitalOcean Spaces API.

spaces_secret_key.description: The secret access key for Spaces API operations.

token.description: The token key for API operations.

active_deployment_id.description: The ID the App's currently active deployment

created_at.description: The date and time of when the App was created

default_ingress.description: The default URL to access the App

deployment_per_page.description: Number of deployments to request per page when polling for deployments

updated_at.description: The date and time of when the App was last updated

urn.description: The uniform resource identifier for the app

id.description: The ID of the dedicated egress IP.

ip.description: The IP address of the dedicated egress IP.

status.description: The status of the dedicated egress IP: 'UNKNOWN', 'ASSIGNING', 'ASSIGNED', or 'REMOVED'

block.description: The dedicated egress IP addresses associated with the app.

disable_edge_cache.description: Whether to disable the edge cache for the app. Default is false, which enables the edge cache.

disable_email_obfuscation.description: Email obfuscation configuration for the app. Default is false, which keeps the email obfuscated.

enhanced_threat_control_enabled.description: Whether to enable enhanced threat control for the app. Default is false. Set to true to enable enhanced threat control, putting additional security measures for Layer 7 DDoS attacks.

features.description: List of features which is applied to the app

name.description: The name of the app. Must be unique across all apps in the same account.

region.description: The slug for the DigitalOcean data center region hosting the app

channel.description: The Slack channel to send notifications to.

cluster_name.description: The name of the underlying DigitalOcean DBaaS cluster. This is required for production databases. For dev databases, if cluster_name is not set, a new cluster will be provisioned.

db_name.description: The name of the MySQL or PostgreSQL database to configure.

db_user.description: The name of the MySQL or PostgreSQL user to configure.

production.description: Whether this is a production or dev database.

version.description: The version of the database engine.

wildcard.description: Indicates whether the domain includes all sub-domains, in addition to the given domain.

zone.description: If the domain uses DigitalOcean DNS and you would like App Platform to automatically manage it for you, set this to the name of the domain on your account.

key.description: The name of the environment variable.

scope.description: The visibility scope of the environment variable.

type.description: The type of the environment variable.

value.description: The value of the environment variable.

source_dir.description: An optional path to the working directory to use for the build.

channel.description: The Slack channel to send notifications to.

block.description: Alert policies for the app component

branch.description: The name of the branch to use.

deploy_on_push.description: Whether to automatically deploy new commits made to the repo

repo.description: The name of the repo in the format `owner/repo`.

allow_credentials.description: Whether browsers should expose the response to the client-side JavaScript code when the request’s credentials mode is `include`. This configures the Access-Control-Allow-Credentials header.

allow_headers.description: The set of allowed HTTP request headers. This configures the Access-Control-Allow-Headers header.

allow_methods.description: The set of allowed HTTP methods. This configures the Access-Control-Allow-Methods header.

expose_headers.description: The set of HTTP response headers that browsers are allowed to access. This configures the Access-Control-Expose-Headers header.

max_age.description: An optional duration specifying how long browsers can cache the results of a preflight request. This configures the Access-Control-Max-Age header. Example: `5h30m`.

block.description: The set of allowed CORS origins. This configures the Access-Control-Allow-Origin header.

success_threshold.description: The number of successful health checks before considered healthy.

timeout_seconds.description: The number of seconds after which the check times out.

Selecting a container instance for termination begins an asynchronous drain of new requests on upstream load-balancers. Default: 15 seconds, Minimum 1, Maximum 110.

certificate_id.description: ID of a DigitalOcean managed TLS certificate for use with custom domains

created_at.description: The date and time (ISO8601) of when the CDN endpoint was created.

custom_domain.description: fully qualified domain name (FQDN) for custom subdomain, (requires certificate_id)

endpoint.description: fully qualified domain name (FQDN) to serve the CDN content

origin.description: fully qualified domain name (FQDN) for the origin server

ttl.description: The amount of time the content is cached in the CDN

ca_cert.description: CA certificate for TLS verification (PEM format)

cluster_id.description: UUID of the source database cluster that will forward logs

id.description: Composite ID of the logsink resource

index_days_max.description: Maximum number of days to retain indices (>= 1)

logsink_id.description: The API sink_id returned by DigitalOcean

timeout_seconds.description: Request timeout for log deliveries in seconds (>= 1)

ca_cert.description: CA certificate for TLS verification (PEM format)

client_key.description: Client private key for mTLS (PEM format)

cluster_id.description: UUID of the source database cluster that will forward logs

format.description: Log format: rfc5424, rfc3164, or custom

id.description: Composite ID of the logsink resource

logline.description: Custom logline template (required when format is 'custom')

logsink_id.description: The API sink_id returned by DigitalOcean

port.description: Port number for the rsyslog server (1-65535)

server.description: Hostname or IP address of the rsyslog server

tls.description: Enable TLS encryption for rsyslog connection

created_at.description: The date and time when the online migration was created

disable_ssl.description: Disables SSL encryption when connecting to the source database

ignore_dbs.description: The list of databases to be ignored during the migration

status.description: The status of the online migration

db_name.description: The name of the default database

host.description: The FQDN pointing to the database cluster's current primary node

port.description: The port on which the database cluster is listening

username.description: The default user of the database

uuid.description: The unique universal identifier for the database replica.

acl_channels_default.description: Determines default pub/sub channels' ACL for new users if ACL is not supplied. When this option is not defined, all_channels is assumed to keep backward compatibility. This option doesn't affect Valkey configuration acl-pubsub-default.

cluster_id.description: A unique identifier for the database cluster.

frequent_snapshots.description: Frequent RDB snapshots. When enabled, Valkey will create frequent local RDB snapshots. When disabled, Valkey will only take RDB snapshots when a backup is created, based on the backup schedule. This setting is ignored when valkey_persistence is set to off.

io_threads.description: The number of IO threads used by Valkey. Must be between 1 and 32.

lfu_decay_time.description: The decay time for Valkey's LFU cache eviction. Must be between 1 and 120.

lfu_log_factor.description: The log factor for Valkey's LFU (Least Frequently Used) cache eviction. Must be between 1 and 100.

notify_keyspace_events.description:
Set notify-keyspace-events option. Requires at least K or E and accepts any combination of the following options. Setting the parameter to "" disables notifications.

K — Keyspace events
E — Keyevent events
g — Generic commands (e.g. DEL, EXPIRE, RENAME, ...)
$ — String commands
l — List commands
s — Set commands
h — Hash commands
z — Sorted set commands
t — Stream commands
d — Module key type events
x — Expired events
e — Evicted events
m — Key miss events
n — New key events
A — Alias for "g$lshztxed"

number_of_databases.description: The number of logical databases in the Valkey cluster. Must be between 1 and 128.

persistence.description: When persistence is 'rdb', Valkey does RDB dumps each 10 minutes if any key is changed. Also RDB dumps are done according to backup schedule for backup purposes. When persistence is 'off', no RDB dumps and backups are done, so data can be lost at any moment if service is restarted for any reason, or if service is powered off. Also service can't be forked.

pubsub_client_output_buffer_limit.description: Set output buffer limit for pub / sub clients in MB. The value is the hard limit, the soft limit is 1/4 of the hard limit. When setting the limit, be mindful of the available memory in the selected service plan.

ssl.description: Whether to enable SSL/TLS for connections to the Valkey cluster.

timeout.description: The timeout (in seconds) for Valkey client connections.

valkey_active_expire_effort.description: Active expire effort. Valkey reclaims expired keys both when accessed and in the background. The background process scans for expired keys to free memory. Increasing the active-expire-effort setting (default 1, max 10) uses more CPU to reclaim expired keys faster, reducing memory usage but potentially increasing latency.

valkey_maxmemory_policy.description: A string specifying the desired eviction policy for a Caching or Valkey cluster.

crawling_option.description:
Options for specifying how URLs found on pages should be handled. 
- UNKNOWN: Default unknown value
- SCOPED: Only include the base URL.
- PATH: Crawl the base URL and linked pages within the URL path.
- DOMAIN: Crawl the base URL and linked pages within the same domain.
- SUBDOMAINS: Crawl the base URL and linked pages for any subdomain.
